published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ + ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML: product-rate/common-EIF orthogonal inference guardrail
none; closest comparators give sufficient DML/LRO/ADML/Riesz/calibration results, not a three-report finite-support pseudo-truth partition

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses closer ADML/Riesz-equivalence comparators: Bruns-Smith et al. 'Augmented balancing weights as linear regression' and Bruns-Smith 2026 'Two Approaches to Direct Estimation of Riesz Representers'.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The LRO definition A_LRO(eta)=E[partial_eta psi_AIPW(O; theta_DML(eta), eta)] is ambiguous: differentiating through theta_DML makes the correction vanish, so the statement must specify theta is held fixed before plugging in the pseudo-target.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite the LRO derivative as a partial derivative with theta held fixed, then define the plug-in pseudo-target separately.
Add Bruns-Smith et al. on augmented balancing weights/AutoDML and Bruns-Smith 2026 on direct Riesz estimators to Related work and the Conjecture 1 comparison block.
Add one explicit J=2 or J=3 symbolic compatibility-cone table showing which cells are non-rectangular before claiming the open-set J<=4 witness.

recommended_next_step: Patch the LRO derivative notation and add the missed Riesz/AutoDML equivalence comparators, then resubmit without changing the core partition kernel.
